vulnerability.title: Enable Smartcards in SSSD
vulnerability.description: SSSD should be configured to authenticate access to the system using smart cards. To enable smart cards in SSSD, set pam_cert_auth to True under the [pam] section in /etc/sssd/sssd.conf. For example: [pam] pam_cert_auth = True Add or update "pam_sss.so" line in auth section of "/etc/pam.d/system-auth" file to include "try_cert_auth" or "require_cert_auth" option, like in the following example: /etc/pam.d/system-auth:auth [success=done authinfo_unavail=ignore ignore=ignore default=die] pam_sss.so try_cert_auth Also add or update "pam_sss.so" line in auth section of "/etc/pam.d/smartcard-auth" file to include the "allow_missing_name" option, like in the following example: /etc/pam.d/smartcard-auth:auth sufficient pam_sss.so allow_missing_name
vulnerability.recommendation:
Remediation script: # Remediation is applicable only in certain platforms if rpm --quiet -q sssd-common; then # sssd configuration files must be created with 600 permissions if they don't exist # otherwise the sssd module fails to start OLD_UMASK=$(umask) umask u=rw,go= found=false # set value in all files if they contain section or key for f in $(echo -n "/etc/sssd/sssd.conf /etc/sssd/conf.d/*.conf"); do if [ ! -e "$f" ]; then continue fi # find key in section and change value if grep -qzosP "[[:space:]]*\[pam\]([^
\[]*
+)+?[[:space:]]*pam_cert_auth" "$f"; then if ! grep -qPz "pam_cert_auth=True" "$f"; then sed -i "s/pam_cert_auth[^(
)]*/pam_cert_auth=True/" "$f" fi found=true # find section and add key = value to it elif grep -qs "[[:space:]]*\[pam\]" "$f"; then sed -i "/[[:space:]]*\[pam\]/a pam_cert_au

triage_decision.reason: Enabling smart card authentication in SSSD follows standard STIG hardening patterns for authentication. The remediation includes fallback options like 'try_cert_auth' to prevent lockout, and the automated script handles configuration safely with proper file permissions.
triage_decision.estimated_impact: authentication/authorization affected; filesystems affected; Misconfiguration could cause authentication failures if smart cards are unavailable; SSSD service restart may temporarily disrupt authentication

reasoning_messages.reasoning:
The finding is about enabling smartcards in SSSD. According to the remediation description:

1. Set `pam_cert_auth = True` under the `[pam]` section in `/etc/sssd/sssd.conf`.
2. Update the `pam_sss.so` line in `/etc/pam.d/system-auth` to include `try_cert_auth` or `require_cert_auth`.
3. Also update `/etc/pam.d/smartcard-auth` (though the description is cut off, it likely needs similar changes).

Let me start by reading the relevant files to understand the current state:
- `/etc/sssd/sssd.conf` - to see if there's a [pam] section and what pam_cert_auth is set to.
- `/etc/pam.d/system-auth` - to see the current pam_sss.so line.
- `/etc/pam.d/smartcard-auth` - to see the current pam_sss.so line.

I'll need to:
1. Read these files first.
2. Then modify them appropriately.

Let me start with reading the files.

reasoning_messages.reasoning:
The user is asking me to remediate an OpenSCAP finding related to enabling smartcards in SSSD. The finding is:

- Title: Enable SmartCards in SSSD
- Rule Name: sssd_enable_smartcards
- Rule ID: xccdf_org.ssgproject.content_rule_sssd_enable_smartcards
- Severity: 2

The remediation steps according to the description:
1. Set pam_cert_auth to True under the [pam] section in /etc/sssd/sssd.conf
2. Update /etc/pam.d/system-auth to include "try_cert_auth" or "require_cert_auth" option in the pam_sss.so line
3. Update /etc/pam.d/smartcard-auth similarly

The previous attempts all tried to read the sssd.conf file but didn't actually make any changes. The scan failed because the remediation wasn't applied.

I need to:
1. First read the current sssd.conf file to see its contents
2. Read the relevant PAM configuration files
3. Plan the modifications needed
4. Apply the changes using write_file or sed

Let me start by reading the sssd.conf file to understand its current state.
